proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 33 features in ds001_crc is monotonically associated with pfs_months at the univariate (Pearson) level with |r| > 0.05.

analyses.code: for c in features: stats.pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate Pearson correlations with pfs_months. Strongest signals: feature_015 r=+0.766, feature_001 r=-0.352, feature_014 r=-0.293, feature_027 r=+0.170, feature_025 r=-0.123, feature_019 r=+0.102, feature_023 r=-0.070, feature_006 r=-0.064. Many weak/null features (|r|<0.02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65 — likely age in years) is positively associated with pfs_months: each 1-unit increase in feature_015 is associated with longer PFS.

analyses.code: df.groupby(pd.qcut(df['feature_015'],10))['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_015 decile rises monotonically from 1.33 months (lowest decile, ~30-52) to 7.45 months (highest decile, ~78-90). Slope ≈ +0.18 PFS-months per unit feature_015. Very strong, monotonic positive relationship; this is the dominant prognostic predictor in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level categorical, values 0/1/2 with prevalences 35%/50%/15% — pattern compatible with ECOG performance status or stage) is inversely associated with pfs_months: PFS decreases as feature_001 rises from 0 → 1 → 2.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_001)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_001=0 → 5.27, =1 → 4.05, =2 → 2.91 months. Differences highly significant (ANOVA F-stat enormous; per-category coefficients in OLS: feature_001=1: -1.22, feature_001=2: -2.36 vs reference 0). Step-down pattern strongly suggests an ordinal severity variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (binary, prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_014=1 have lower mean PFS than those with feature_014=0.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_014']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_014']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.70 (feature_014=1, n=27481) vs 5.05 (feature_014=0, n=22519). Difference = -1.35 months, t = -68.6, p ≈ 0. Strong, robust prognostic effect; in adjusted multivariable OLS the coefficient remains ≈ -1.36 (p ≈ 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous lab-like features, feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8 — pattern consistent with serum albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months and feature_025 (range 0-25, mean 3.8 — could be number of metastatic sites or LDH/bilirubin) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_019 + feature_025', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_019: coef = +0.47 PFS-months per unit (p<1e-100). feature_025: coef = -0.08 PFS-months per unit (p<1e-100). Both directions confirmed with high significance; both effects survive adjustment in the full multivariable OLS model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all 33 features captures the bulk of explainable variance (R² > 0.8) and reveals which features are independently prognostic vs. confounded.

analyses.code: X = df.drop(columns=['patient_id','pfs_months']); sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS on all 33 features yields R² = 0.859. Significant independent predictors: feature_015 (+0.176), feature_001 (-1.17), feature_014 (-1.36), feature_027 (+0.94), feature_006 (-0.29), feature_023 (-0.35), feature_022 (-0.36), feature_005 (-0.005), feature_019 (+0.47), feature_025 (-0.08), feature_021 (-0.0004). Many candidate features (feature_002, _010, _012, _016, _018, _020, _024, _028, _031, plus most lab features) carry no independent signal — purely noise/null variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, prevalence 20%) is the dataset's treatment indicator: patients with feature_027=1 have meaningfully longer pfs_months than patients with feature_027=0 (positive direction, ≈ +1 month overall).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_027']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_027']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.09 (feature_027=1, n=10022) vs 4.12 (feature_027=0, n=39978). Difference = +0.97 months, t = +38.5, p ≈ 0. Adjusted effect in full OLS = +0.94 (p ≈ 0). Among all binary features, feature_027 is the only one with both a moderate prevalence (~20%) and a positive PFS effect — the natural treatment-arm candidate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across binary features: at least one binary marker eliminates or reverses the benefit. Run a treatment × binary-feature interaction screen across all binary features.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_027 * {c}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction screen ranked by p-value. Strong negative interactions (each abolishes feature_027 benefit when =1): feature_023 (interaction = -1.63, p=7.7e-226), feature_006 (-1.45, p=4e-168), feature_022 (-1.18, p=6e-23). One positive interaction: feature_011 (+0.99, p=1.2e-11). All other binary features have non-significant interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months: in feature_023=0 patients, feature_027 yields a substantial PFS gain; in feature_023=1 patients, feature_027 has no effect.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query('feature_023==@v & feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], df.query('feature_023==@v & feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: feature_027 effect = +1.66 months (n_trt=5816, n_ctrl=23286, p≈0). feature_023=1: feature_027 effect = +0.027 months (n_trt=4206, n_ctrl=16692, p=0.45). Complete abolition of treatment benefit when feature_023=1 — qualitative interaction, not just attenuation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006=1 abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months, independently of feature_023.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query('feature_006==@v & feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], df.query('feature_006==@v & feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: feature_027 effect = +1.48 months (n_trt=6524, n_ctrl=25819, p≈0). feature_006=1: feature_027 effect = +0.026 months (n_trt=3498, n_ctrl=14159, p=0.52). feature_006=1 is also a complete-resistance marker for the treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022=1 (rare, 4.5% prevalence) abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query('feature_022==@v & feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], df.query('feature_022==@v & feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0: feature_027 effect = +1.03 months (n_trt=9554, n_ctrl=38174, p≈0). feature_022=1: feature_027 effect = -0.16 months (n_trt=468, n_ctrl=1804, p=0.15). feature_022=1 also abolishes (and possibly mildly reverses) the treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 enhances (does not just preserve) the feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months: the absolute PFS gain in feature_011=1 patients exceeds that in feature_011=0 patients.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query('feature_011==@v & feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], df.query('feature_011==@v & feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0: feature_027 effect = +0.94 months (n_trt=9709, p≈0). feature_011=1: feature_027 effect = +1.93 months (n_trt=313, n_ctrl=1193, p=1.5e-35). The doubling of effect size is statistically significant (interaction coef = +0.99, p=1.2e-11), but it disappears once feature_023, feature_006, feature_022 are conditioned on (see h13.1) — feature_011 is not an independent modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the wild-type triple-negative subgroup (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0) derive a much larger feature_027 treatment benefit (>2 months PFS gain) than the overall cohort.

analyses.code: sub = df[(df.feature_023==0)&(df.feature_006==0)&(df.feature_022==0)]; stats.ttest_ind(sub.query('feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], sub.query('feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Triple-WT subgroup n=17372 (35% of cohort). feature_027 effect = +2.76 months (n_trt=3461 mean=6.88, n_ctrl=13911 mean=4.12, t≈100, p≈0). Effect size nearly tripled relative to overall +0.97. feature_023, feature_006, feature_022 act as parallel/complementary resistance markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-WT subgroup, treatment effect of feature_027 is further modified by a continuous feature. Run treatment × continuous-feature interaction screen restricted to the triple-WT subgroup.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_027 * {c}', sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple-WT subgroup, feature_005 emerges as a dominant continuous modifier of feature_027: interaction coef = -0.065 PFS-months per unit feature_005 (p = 7.4e-136). All other continuous interactions are weak or null (next-best feature_017 p=0.028 with tiny coef). feature_005 is right-skewed (range 0-440, median 4.5 in this subgroup) — consistent with a tumor-burden marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-WT subgroup, treatment effect of feature_027 declines monotonically with feature_005, with a sharp loss of benefit somewhere between feature_005 = 4 and feature_005 = 8.

analyses.code: for q in pd.qcut(sub['feature_005'],5): subq = ...; ttest
analyses.result_summary: Triple-WT, feature_005 quintile, treatment vs control diff: Q1 (0.04-1.61) +5.02, Q2 (1.61-3.27) +5.06, Q3 (3.27-6.02) +3.45, Q4 (6.02-12.18) -0.01, Q5 (12.18+) -0.02. Sharp transition between Q3 and Q4 — treatment benefit collapses to zero above feature_005 ≈ 6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The optimal feature_005 cutoff for predicting feature_027 benefit in the triple-WT subgroup is approximately 6: below this threshold the treatment effect is large (≈ +4.5 months), at/above it the treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: for thr in [3,4,4.5,5,5.5,6,6.5,7,8,9,10]: split sub on feature_005<thr; compare diff(trt-ctrl)
analyses.result_summary: Split table (LOW/HIGH treatment-effect difference at each cutoff): thr=3 +5.03/+1.33; thr=5 +5.01/+0.005; thr=6 +4.55/-0.02; thr=7 +4.13/+0.02; thr=8 +3.90/-0.004. The treatment-effect step is sharp around feature_005 ∈ [5,6]: above it, effect is essentially zero; below it, effect is ≈ +4.5-5 months. Cutoff = 6 chosen as a clean operational threshold with HIGH-side effect indistinguishable from 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Define the eligible subgroup as feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<6. Within eligible patients (≈ 21% of cohort), feature_027 yields a very large positive PFS benefit (≈ +4.5 months); within ineligible patients, feature_027 has no effect on PFS.

analyses.code: df['eligible'] = ((df.feature_023==0)&(df.feature_006==0)&(df.feature_022==0)&(df.feature_005<6)).astype(int); for e in [1,0]: ttest
analyses.result_summary: Eligible n=10398 (20.8% of cohort): feature_027 effect = +4.55 months (n_trt=2106 mean=8.63, n_ctrl=8292 mean=4.09, p≈0). Ineligible n=39602: feature_027 effect = +0.022 months (n_trt=7916, n_ctrl=31686, p=0.41). Joint interaction model: feature_027:eligible coef = +4.52 (p<1e-300); main effect of feature_027 alone is null (p=0.41). Complete predictive separation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, the feature_027 treatment effect is further modified by feature_001 (3-category severity): higher-severity patients derive smaller treatment benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_001)', sub_eligible).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible subgroup, feature_027 main coef = +4.40, feature_027:feature_001=1 = +0.21 (p=0.05), feature_027:feature_001=2 = +0.03 (p=0.85). No clinically meaningful effect-modification by feature_001 within the eligible subgroup — treatment benefit is approximately uniform across severity strata once eligibility is satisfied.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, no remaining binary feature significantly modifies the feature_027 treatment effect at the |coef|>0.4 / p<0.001 level.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_027 * {c}', sub_eligible).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Sweep of treatment×binary interactions inside the eligible subgroup. Only feature_031 yields p<0.05 (interaction coef = +0.27, p=0.011) — small magnitude, likely noise after multiplicity. feature_014 trend (+0.24, p=0.005) similar. No additional resistance/responder splits; the four-way eligibility predicate captures the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, no remaining continuous feature significantly modifies the feature_027 treatment effect at the p<0.001 level (i.e., feature_005 was the unique continuous modifier and its variation has been fully exploited by the <6 threshold).

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols (excluding feature_005): smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_027 * {c}', sub_eligible).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Sweep of treatment×continuous interactions inside the eligible subgroup. No interaction reaches p<0.05; feature_005 itself is no longer modifier within the eligible subgroup (range now 0-6). Confirms eligibility predicate is complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable OLS for pfs_months including the eligibility×treatment interaction and main prognostic factors, the eligibility-conditional treatment effect remains ≈ +4.5 months and the unconditioned feature_027 main effect is null.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*eligible + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_005 + feature_021', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model (R² = 0.976): feature_027:eligible = +4.51 (p<1e-300), feature_027 main = -0.004 (p=0.37), eligible main = -0.006 (p=0.20). Confirmed prognostic factors: feature_015 +0.176, feature_001=1 -1.18, feature_001=2 -2.32, feature_014 -1.37, feature_019 +0.48, feature_025 -0.078. Treatment-effect estimate is robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ineligible subgroup the feature_027 treatment effect adjusted for prognostic factors is statistically indistinguishable from zero (no hidden benefit recovered by adjustment).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014', df.query('eligible==0')).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_027 coef in ineligible subgroup = -0.0073 (SE 0.005, p=0.18). Direction trivially negative; magnitude trivially small. Conclusion: feature_027 confers zero benefit (and certainly no harm worth treating with) outside the eligibility subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature in the dataset behaves like a treatment with subgroup-specific effects (i.e., feature_027 is the unique treatment indicator). Verify by screening every binary feature for any treatment×marker-style interaction with magnitude comparable to feature_027's profile.

analyses.code: for trt_cand in binary_cols: for mod in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {trt_cand}*{mod}', df).fit(); record largest |coef| with p<0.001
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise interaction sweep across all binary features. Only feature_027 shows significant large-magnitude interactions (|coef|>0.3, p<1e-10) — and those are exactly with feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_011. All other binary features show no significant interactions (best p > 0.05 after the feature_027 partners are excluded). feature_027 is the unique treatment-like variable; the remaining binaries are prognostic or null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (rare, 3% prevalence) does not provide any additional treatment-effect modification once the eligibility predicate (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<6) is satisfied — i.e., feature_011 is not an independent biomarker of feature_027 benefit.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query('eligible==1 & feature_011==@v & feature_027==1')['pfs_months'], df.query('eligible==1 & feature_011==@v & feature_027==0')['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible subgroup, feature_011=0: feature_027 effect = +4.53 (n_trt=1985, p≈0). feature_011=1: feature_027 effect = +4.85 (n_trt=121, p=4.5e-76). Difference between subgroups (+0.32) is not significantly different from 0 in adjusted models. The earlier feature_011 × feature_027 interaction (+0.99 unadjusted) was driven entirely by enrichment of triple-WT/low-feature_005 patients within feature_011=1, not by independent biomarker activity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS. Treatment with feature_027 increases pfs_months by approximately +4.5 months exclusively among patients in the eligible subgroup defined by feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<6 (which represents ~21% of the cohort). Patients failing any one of these four criteria derive zero PFS benefit from feature_027 (adjusted effect indistinguishable from 0). The predictive subgroup is therefore conjunctive: three binary resistance markers plus a continuous tumor-burden / load marker each acting as an absolute requirement for response.

analyses.code: df['eligible'] = ((df.feature_023==0)&(df.feature_006==0)&(df.feature_022==0)&(df.feature_005<6)).astype(int); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*eligible + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final fully adjusted model: feature_027:eligible interaction = +4.51 (95% CI 4.49 to 4.53, p<1e-300). feature_027 main = essentially 0 (p=0.37). Eligible-only adjusted feature_027 effect = +4.50 (95% CI 4.47-4.53, p<1e-300, R²=0.94). Ineligible-only adjusted feature_027 effect = -0.007 (p=0.18). Subgroup definition is complete: removing any one of the four predicates re-introduces a substantial untreated/eligible mismatch, while adding any further feature does not improve the partition.
